public_functions.behavior_description: Enable the GTM and its functional clocks, then configure a shared TOM time base at 20 kHz with center-aligned operation. Initialize the timer configuration with the selected FXCLK source, TOM instance, and timer channel. Route six PWM output channels by assigning each channel's cfg.pin.outputPin in the IfxGtm_Tom_Pwm_Config (no separate pin map API call). Build a unified multi-channel PWM configuration: assign six single-output channels under the same time base, bind each logical channel to the selected TOM submodule and physical channel, and enable synchronized updates. Set polarity in the IfxGtm_Tom_Pwm_Config so that the three high-side channels use the active HIGH polarity while the three low-side channels use inverted polarity to implement complementary behavior with zero dead time. Set initial duty values in the IfxGtm_Tom_Pwm_Config: high-side channels at 25%, 50%, and 75%; low-side channels match the same numeric duty values because their inversion is realized by polarity (yielding complementary waveforms). After initialization, call IfxGtm_Tom_Pwm_start, then perform a shadowed update sequence: disable timer updates, write the initial six-channel duty array in one call, and apply the update atomically so all channels start synchronized.

Maintain six duty values corresponding to the configured PWM channels. Track elapsed time across calls; only every 500 ms perform an update. On update: increase each high-side duty by +10 percentage points (i.e., +0.10 in normalized scale). If a high-side duty exceeds 100% (greater than 1.0), wrap it to 0.0. For complementary behavior without hardware dead time, set the low-side numeric duties to the same values as their corresponding high-side channels (the effective complement is achieved by the inverted polarity configured during initialization). Assemble the six-duty array in channel order, disable timer updates to stage a coherent transfer, invoke the multi-channel immediate duty update to push all six values together, and then apply the update atomically so all outputs change in sync.

header_selection.reconciliation_reason: LLM driver_calls use IfxGtm_Tom_* API, not IfxGtm_Tom_Timer (the reference API). Updated to match actual usage.
